message:
🐛 Fix GA4 0s engagement time and blocked OpenShift analytics (#1655)

Two bugs causing GA4 to report 0s Average Engagement Time on all pages
and zero analytics from OpenShift deployments:

1. Missing user_engagement event: GA4 calculates engagement time
   exclusively from user_engagement events. Our custom implementation
   tracked engagement correctly but never emitted this event type.
   Now emits user_engagement on visibility change, beforeunload,
   and SPA navigation.

2. Origin validation blocking OpenShift: allowedOrigins only had
   localhost, 127.0.0.1, and console.kubestellar.io. OpenShift
   hostnames (vllm-d, pok-prod) were rejected with 403. Added
   same-origin check so any deployment serving the console accepts
   its own analytics requests.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
